c.d: The range of operating systems and devices on which the app is available (e.g., Windows, macOS, Android, iOS).
c.s: Official app websites, app store listings

c.d: The variety and depth of functionalities offered by the app, such as support for multimedia, collaboration tools, and organizational capabilities.
c.s: Official app websites, user reviews, tech blogs

c.d: The design, ease of use, and overall user experience provided by the app.
c.s: User reviews, tech blogs, app store ratings

c.d: The app's ability to sync notes across devices and integrate with cloud services for backup and accessibility.
The measures taken by the app to ensure user data is secure and private, including encryption and compliance with privacy standards.
c.s: Official app websites, privacy policies, security audits

c.d: The cost structure of the app, including free tiers, subscription plans, and one-time purchases.
c.s: Official app websites, app store listings

c.d: The availability of user communities, forums, and official support channels for assistance and sharing best practices.
c.s: Official app websites, user forums, tech blogs
